title: Vegetables not shown to be beneficial

chunks.title: Introduction to Vegetable Studies
chunks.summary: The speaker begins by discussing the multitude of studies highlighting the benefits of vegetables in the human diet, questioning the validity of observational studies. They express skepticism towards the notion that eating more vegetables is associated with better health outcomes, emphasizing the need for interventional studies.
chunks.transcript: but Paul you say there are so many studies showing the benefits of vegetables in the human diet oh yeah show me because the best meta-analysis that I can find of vegetables is this one the effects of fruit and vegetable consumption on the inflammatory biomarkers and immune cell populations a systematic literature review and meta-analysis now notice they say fruit and vegetable consumption so I will walk you through all of these studies this is going to be a little bit hard to read but there are many studies they reviewed here and they're all right here all the references will be there all of them are summarized here now the first set of studies are all about fruit I started this journey as a carnivore and didn't think that fruit was good for humans that opinion that idea changed when I added carbohydrates back to my diet and now it makes sense to me that fruit is something that we would seek as humans that would be less toxic for us less full of problematic chemicals we know that fruit has less defense chemicals as it ripens so studies looking at fruit extracts and human health are not what I'm interested in what we are interested in this discussion is vegetables and their effect on human health and we must note that epidemiology observational literature here is going to be heavily confounded by healthy user bias in this situation so I'm not terribly convinced by an observational study that says that if you eat more vegetables that's associated with Better Health outcome because those people are likely to do other good health behaviors what I want to see are Interventional studies with vegetables that result in good health outcomes for humans those are not very large in number so all of you out there saying there are tons of studies showing the vegetables are good for humans um I would ask you to share them with me because there's not a ton out there

chunks.title: Confounding Factors in Observational Studies
chunks.summary: The speaker elaborates on the healthy user bias present in observational studies, asserting that such studies often do not convincingly demonstrate the benefits of vegetable consumption due to confounding factors. They argue for a more rigorous examination of interventional studies to ascertain the true impact of vegetable intake on health.
chunks.transcript: actually when I went on the doctors they certainly railroaded me Travis Stork said there are thousands of studies where where Travis Stork so let's look at this paper and try and be balanced here you can see that the majority of the studies in the beginning here are with fruits so we'll skip over all the fruit studies pomegranate grape okay etc etc orange juice raisins tomato

chunks.title: Overview of Existing Research
chunks.summary: The speaker reviews various interventions, detailing specific studies on vegetables and their failed effects on inflammatory markers in the diet. They note that despite claims of benefits from vegetable consumption, many trials yield underwhelming results regarding health outcomes.

chunks.title: Skepticism Towards Current Evidence
chunks.summary: The speaker expresses skepticism towards the overwhelming claim that vegetables are beneficial for human health and challenges listeners to provide evidence supporting this notion. They highlight the ambiguity in findings and urge for a more critical approach to the existing literature.
chunks.transcript: benefits at all and I'll show you some of those so where exactly is the experimental evidence that is so overwhelming that vegetables are good for humans I just I don't see it that there may be a trial hero trial there and we can match that with another trial that shows there's no benefits so I think that if you look at the literature there is no clear signal that vegetables are good for humans if you guys don't agree with that I'm open to comments and future debates I've done them in the past with people but I think that this is an interesting thing to really look at the literature somewhat objectively
